--- title: "第六次课-列表与数据框的操作" --- ::: {.cell} :::
# do.call 函数（列表的操作） do.call () executes any function by its name's when a list of the corresponding argument is passed ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} list1 <- list(c(1, 2, 3), c(7, 5, 10), c(9, 9, 2)) #sum(list1) error do.call(sum, list1) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] 48 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} do.call(sum, list1[1:2]) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] 28 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} #do.call(mean,list1) df1 <- data.frame(team=c('A', 'B', 'C'), points=c(22, 27, 38)) df2 <- data.frame(team=c('D', 'E', 'F'), points=c(22, 14, 20)) df3 <- data.frame(team=c('G', 'H', 'I'), points=c(11, 15, 18)) #place three data frames into list df_list <- list(df1, df2, df3) do.call(rbind, df_list) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` team points 1 A 22 2 B 27 3 C 38 4 D 22 5 E 14 6 F 20 7 G 11 8 H 15 9 I 18 ``` ::: ::: # apply家族函数（单一数据结构的贯穿式操作） -- apply(x, MARGIN, FUN, ...) x：数组或矩阵 MARGIN：取一个介于1到2之间的值或范围，以定义该函数的应用位置： -MARGIN = 1：对行执行操作 -MARGIN = 2：对列执行操作 -MARGIN = c（1,2）该操作在行和列上执行 -FUN：告诉应用哪个功能。可以应用平均值，中位数，和，最小值，最大值甚至用户定义的函数等内置函数 --lapply(X, FUN) Arguments: -X: A vector or an object -FUN: Function applied to each element of x -- sapply(X, FUN, ..., simplify = TRUE, USE.NAMES = TRUE) -- tapply(X, INDEX, FUN = NULL) Arguments: -- mapply(FUN, ..., MoreArgs = NULL, SIMPLIFY = TRUE, USE.NAMES = TRUE) 直接上代码解释 ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} X<-matrix(rnorm(30), nrow=5, ncol=6) apply(X,1 ,sum)#row sum ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] -2.371107 3.434113 -1.931315 -3.390450 -4.747841 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} apply(X, 2, sort) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] [,6] [1,] -0.77230220 -0.8359501 -1.1837118 -1.20079059 -2.0124956 -0.47894423 [2,] -0.76556911 -0.4429546 -1.1255063 -1.06164295 -1.2062982 -0.12429692 [3,] -0.04169066 -0.3378816 -0.9802672 -0.31580760 -1.1235364 -0.05862774 [4,] 0.32529755 0.1281910 -0.1601093 -0.07660451 -1.0912453 0.11385060 [5,] 1.85054280 1.0559509 1.1934856 1.45638848 -0.2283105 0.49423554 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} apply(X, 2, is.vector) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] TRUE TRUE TRUE TRUE TRUE TRUE ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} A<-matrix(1:9, 3,3) B<-matrix(4:15, 4,3) C<-matrix(8:10, 3,2) MyList<-list(A,B,C) # display the list lapply(MyList,"[", , 2)# [,2] ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [[1]] [1] 4 5 6 [[2]] [1] 8 9 10 11 [[3]] [1] 8 9 10 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} lapply(MyList,"[", 1, )# [1,] ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [[1]] [1] 1 4 7 [[2]] [1] 4 8 12 [[3]] [1] 8 8 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} sapply(MyList,"[", 2,1)# 向量化结果 ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] 2 5 9 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} movies <- c("SPYDERMAN","BATMAN","VERTIGO","CHINATOWN") lapply(movies, tolower) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [[1]] [1] "spyderman" [[2]] [1] "batman" [[3]] [1] "vertigo" [[4]] [1] "chinatown" ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} sapply(cars, min) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` speed dist 4 2 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} tapply(iris$Sepal.Width, iris$Species, median) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` setosa versicolor virginica 3.4 2.8 3.0 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} A = list(c(1, 2, 3, 4)) B = list(c(2, 5, 1, 6)) mapply(sum, A, B) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] 24 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} mapply(rep,1:4,4:1) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [[1]] [1] 1 1 1 1 [[2]] [1] 2 2 2 [[3]] [1] 3 3 [[4]] [1] 4 ``` ::: ::: # sweep函数（两个数据结构的对应操作） 数据标准化为例： $x_{std}=\frac{x-\bar{x}}{x_{sd}}$ ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} df <- iris[,1:4] col.mean <- apply(df,2,mean) col.sd <- apply(df,2,sd) df.mean.sub <- sweep(df,2,col.mean,"-") df.std <- sweep(df.mean.sub,2,col.sd,"/") apply(df.std,2,sum) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Sepal.Length Sepal.Width Petal.Length Petal.Width -6.721013e-14 3.053113e-14 -4.267420e-15 -4.572731e-15 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} .Machine$double.eps ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] 2.220446e-16 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} #the smallest positive floating-point number ``` ::: # aggregate 函数(分类汇总) ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} aggregate(mtcars[,1],list(mtcars$am,mtcars$cyl),mean) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Group.1 Group.2 x 1 0 4 22.90000 2 1 4 28.07500 3 0 6 19.12500 4 1 6 20.56667 5 0 8 15.05000 6 1 8 15.40000 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} aggregate(mpg~am*cyl,data = mtcars,mean) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` am cyl mpg 1 0 4 22.90000 2 1 4 28.07500 3 0 6 19.12500 4 1 6 20.56667 5 0 8 15.05000 6 1 8 15.40000 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} aggregate(. ~ Species, data = iris, mean) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Species Sepal.Length Sepal.Width Petal.Length Petal.Width 1 setosa 5.006 3.428 1.462 0.246 2 versicolor 5.936 2.770 4.260 1.326 3 virginica 6.588 2.974 5.552 2.026 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} ag <- aggregate(len ~ ., data = ToothGrowth, mean) xtabs(len ~ ., data = ag) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` dose supp 0.5 1 2 OJ 13.23 22.70 26.06 VC 7.98 16.77 26.14 ``` ::: ::: # map函数家族 The map() function from the purrr package in R can be used to apply some function to each element in a vector or list and return a list as a result. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(purrr) data <- list(1L, "a", FALSE) map(data, typeof) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [[1]] [1] "integer" [[2]] [1] "character" [[3]] [1] "logical" ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} list.A <- list(c(1, 2, 3), c(4, 5, 6), c(7, 8, NA)) map(list.A, sum,na.rm=TRUE) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [[1]] [1] 6 [[2]] [1] 15 [[3]] [1] 15 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} map(iris[,1:4],mean) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` $Sepal.Length [1] 5.843333 $Sepal.Width [1] 3.057333 $Petal.Length [1] 3.758 $Petal.Width [1] 1.199333 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} map(1:3,rnorm,n=5) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [[1]] [1] 2.8621688 0.5705823 1.9186709 1.0043850 1.5840106 [[2]] [1] 0.6816606 1.4314753 0.1256303 1.8433423 0.6083949 [[3]] [1] 2.716417 2.735687 3.567346 1.234519 1.698923 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} map(1:3,function(x) rnorm(5, x)) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [[1]] [1] 2.1963155 1.5492779 1.7521197 0.8813378 -1.0614615 [[2]] [1] 0.2319628 1.7830269 2.0631636 1.2314301 2.7598050 [[3]] [1] 3.011321 3.926830 2.660265 3.084080 3.720410 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} map(1:3,~rnorm(5, .x)) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [[1]] [1] -0.8053599 0.4174787 -0.5283062 2.9649473 1.6452803 [[2]] [1] 2.459791 2.339766 2.543862 2.226095 4.001720 [[3]] [1] 2.372331 3.305904 3.326281 2.604747 4.143392 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} l2 <- list( list(num = 1:3, letters[1:3]), list(num = 101:103, letters[4:6]), list() ) map(l2,c(2, 2)) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [[1]] [1] "b" [[2]] [1] "e" [[3]] NULL ``` ::: ::: # R数据变形的有力工具 ![图6-1：reshape包中melt和cast函数的用法示意图](H:/上课材料/R语言编程基础/R 语言第二次2021年春夏/material figures/reshaping.png){ width=80% }
